_source: Tang et al., Geometric indices from CT for evaluating OA severity in mice (OA-uCT paper).
_provenance: PROVENANCE MATTERS — two kinds of value here. (1) RATIO thresholds (femur W/L 1.28/1.30; tibial IIOC height/width 0.28) are stated VERBATIM in the paper's Results text and are exact. (2) mm-level ranges (femur length/width, tibial width, IIOC height, GP thickness) are NOT in the text; they are read approximately off the Fig 2/3/4 scatter-plot AXES by eye and are therefore soft estimates, not quoted numbers. Treat the mm 'gate' bounds as generous sanity limits (catch gross errors like a 2x-off length), NOT as tight confidence intervals. Re-read the figures or replace with your own study's values when precision matters.
_use: Gate every auto-proposed measurement against these ranges. A value outside its gate is presumptively wrong -> run another detection-loop iteration. Ranges are for adult C57BL/6 mouse knee; widen/replace when calibrating to another study or species.

distal_femur_length_mm.note: FIGURE-READ (Fig 2E/4A scatter axes, ~2.3-2.4 mm central; NOT a text-quoted number). Text confirms only that length 'remains unchanged' and serves as a stable reference denominator. Gate is deliberately wider than the ~2.3-2.4 central cloud so it catches gross errors (the 1.08 mm heuristic failure) without over-tuning to an eyeballed figure value; a passing value near 2.4 is consistent, not pinpoint-validated.

distal_femur_width_mm.note: Fig 2B / 4A: ~2.8-2.9 normal, rises to ~3.2-3.5 with osteophytes. Increases with OA.

femur_width_length_ratio.note: THE primary femoral OA index. <1.28 normal, >1.30 osteoarthritic. Aged OA joints reach >1.33. This gate catches a broken length even when width is right.

tibial_width_mm.note: Fig 3B / 4B: ~2.7-3.0 mm; increases with OA osteophyte formation.

tibial_IIOC_max_height_mm.note: Fig 3C / 4B: ~0.8-0.9 mm; decreases with subchondral collapse.

tibial_IIOC_height_width_ratio.note: Second primary index (tibia). Paper states a SINGLE cutoff: >0.28 normal, <0.28 osteoarthritic (0.27 appears only as a descriptive statistic, not a threshold).

compartment_height_mm.note: Medial/lateral IIOC compartment heights, plateau midpoint to growth-plate/epiphyseal line. Fig 3D/3E scatter. Medial and lateral should be COMPARABLE in a normal joint; a large medial>>lateral asymmetry on a normal control suggests the growth-plate boundary was placed too deep on one side.

growth_plate_thickness_mm.note: Mouse proximal-tibial growth plate ~0.15-0.25 mm. A segmented sheet much thicker than this is swallowing marrow.

voxel_size_um.note: Scanco VivaCT 40, 10.5 um isotropic. Scanner thresholds are UNITLESS (220 bone/soft-tissue, 320 for 3D, 270 cortical/plate). The >2500 HU threshold is the separate Amira pipeline, not Scanco.
